title: Recurrent tonsillitis

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting recurrent sore-throat episodes attributed by a clinician to acute tonsillitis, episode history, normal-function impact, work or school impact, and complications without applying an automatic procedural threshold.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact EBI section supports episode history, onset and recurrence pattern, symptom and functional impact, associated tonsillitis features, relevant negatives, concerns, and clinician-performed ENT or general assessment without applying a procedural threshold.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting recurrent sore-throat episodes attributed by a clinician to acute tonsillitis, episode history, normal-function impact, work or school impact, and complications without applying an automatic procedural threshold.

exact_section.heading: Summary and recommendation — recurrent episodes, documentation, and functional impact
exact_section.locator: official guidance Summary and Recommendation sections

red_flags.text: Supports recording a clinician-completed benefit-risk-alternative discussion and recognizing sleep-disordered breathing, suspected cancer, recurrent quinsy, emergency presentations, immune deficiency, and other specialist contexts without recommending surgery.
red_flags.rationale: The exact shared-decision and exclusions section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending tonsillectomy or setting a threshold.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports recording a clinician-completed benefit-risk-alternative discussion and recognizing sleep-disordered breathing, suspected cancer, recurrent quinsy, emergency presentations, immune deficiency, and other specialist contexts without recommending surgery.

exact_section.heading: Recommendation and exclusions — shared decision and separate pathways
exact_section.locator: official guidance Recommendation and exclusion list

red_flags.text: Lists airway, swallowing, breathing, pain, immune compromise, neck stiffness, dehydration, and complication prompts for escalation.
red_flags.rationale: The exact DHA red-flag section was reviewed for acute safety context; no automatic escalation or referral text was mapped.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists airway, swallowing, breathing, pain, immune compromise, neck stiffness, dehydration, and complication prompts for escalation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis (Sore Throat)

investigations.text: Lists swallowing pain, fever, cough and URTI features, tonsillar findings, cervical nodes, rash, and clinician-selected GAS testing context.
investigations.rationale: The exact DHA sore-throat features and testing section was reviewed for UAE acute-episode context, but no diagnostic score or automatic test interpretation was mapped.
investigations.evidence_extract: Lists swallowing pain, fever, cough and URTI features, tonsillar findings, cervical nodes, rash, and clinician-selected GAS testing context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis (Sore Throat)

source_limitations: Age, verified episode count and dates, fever, nodes, exudate, testing, treatment, school or work loss, quinsy, sleep symptoms, immune disease, examination, diagnosis, surgery discussion, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
